_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to answer simple questions about a story or text we listened to or watched together.

_source.w: We are learning to answer simple questions about a story or text we listened to or watched together.
y: Answering questions shows we were paying attention and understanding the story.
Practising responses helps us get better at sharing our ideas with others.
Thinking about questions deepens our understanding of what we hear and see.
eg: We answer 'Where does the bird live?' by pointing to a picture of a tree.
We use our device to answer 'Who was in the story?' by selecting the right character.
We nod or shake our head to answer yes/no questions about a shared text.
We choose from two pictures to answer a 'what' question about the story.
We use signs or gestures to respond when asked about an event in the text.
We select the right image to show what happened in the story.
